title: Darb v2 — Product Requirements Document
summary: Pivot PRD reframing Darb from a multi-platform fleet dashboard into an AI ops chief for Gulf delivery fleet owners, centered on an agent-driven Decisions/Chat/Floor surface with a propose-and-confirm action moat.
scope: AI agent (Decisions, Chat, Live Floor surfaces)
Cross-platform fleet ops (Keeta, Talabat, Deliveroo, Americana)
Finance module (Cash, Payroll, Invoices, Expenses & P&L)
Agent tool surface (read tools and action tools)
Autonomy model (propose-and-confirm v1, partial auto-execute v2, standing rules v3)
Data architecture additions (AgentAction, AgentMemory, PinnedView, PerformanceSnapshot, AgentRule)
Mobile app as cross-platform GPS telemetry beacon
Scraping adapter layer and XLSX fallback
12-month roadmap (Q1 Wedge, Q2 Watchtower & Cash, Q3 Trust & Autonomy v2, Q4 Standing Rules & GCC prep)
Bilingual strategy (English-first UI, Arabic outbound courier comms)
notes: Manifest declared PRD with precedence 0 (highest). Frontmatter explicitly states 'Status: Draft v0.1 — for founder review' and contains explicit 'Decisions I need from you this week' section, so requirements should be treated as proposed/draft (not locked). Document is structurally a PRD: vision, ICP/personas, principles, surfaces, roadmap, pricing/GTM, risks, non-goals, open questions. It also contains numerous decision-style assertions (pivot framing, English-first, propose-and-confirm autonomy, KD 2/courier pricing, hide-behind-flag plan) that are pending founder approval — synthesizer should treat these as PROPOSED requirements/decisions, not LOCKED. Strong cross-references to existing codebase services (aiChiefOfStaffService, aiScoringService, aiAnomalyService) and existing routes (Keeta /import, keeta/violations) — the pivot explicitly preserves backend foundations and proposes deprecating most frontend pages behind a feature flag. Scope boundaries are unusually explicit: section 12 lists explicit non-goals for 12 months.
